candidates.stmt: Mark was born and raised in Tipton, attending Tipton Green Juniors and Alexandra High School before becoming the first in his family to attend University, studying Business Accounting at Glamorgan. Following University he moved to London where he carved out a successful career in media finance, rising to become the Finance Director of the company that produces the Great British Bake Off. It was whilst in London that Mark first became involved in politics, joining the Lib Dems in 2017 and campaigning in local and national elections.

My name is Terry Wing and I am the Reform UK candidate for the ward of Binstead and Fishbourne.
I began my working life as a 15-year-old apprentice. I joined the Territorial Army at 17 and had a 38-year military career (reserve and regular) including an operational tour in Iraq. After retiring I managed the UK-wide Volunteer Cadet
Corps (founded 1901, for ages 8-18) and became its first Lt Colonel, Commander VCC reporting to the 2nd Sea Lord.
I spent several years as a school governor at a special needs school providing advice on funding and specialist building facilities. At the same time, I became a trustee of the Duke of Connaught Trust; providing funding and assistance to
reserve forces and cadet organisation in the Hampshire and Isle of Wight areas.
Five years ago, I retired from the corps and bought a farm on the Island. My wife and I run a smallholding and a holiday business with 3 cottages. As many of you undoubtedly are too, we are currently facing challenges such as rising costs,
poor roads and access issues on the Island.
I am a firm believer that the Island offers a unique work/life balance and I hope you will give me the opportunity to utilise the qualities of loyalty, courage, determination, respect, integrity and commitment I gained throughout my life in
the voluntary sector, business and my military career to help improve the opportunities and quality of life of Binstead and Fishbourne residents
I ask for your support on 7th May for Binstead and Fishbou

Alice grew up on the Island, worked in a local restaurant and volunteered with the scouts before going to university. Alice says “I understand the challenges of local people and islanders, especially those of young people and members of the LGBTQ+ Community. My priorities will be improving local education, creating better public transport, protecting our green spaces, and standing up for all Islanders.”

Drawing on my past experiences as a Councillor at County and Parish level, I want to pursue changes to the current system which currently discourages many able people from getting involved in local politics. I also want to help develop a closer relationship between residents, parish and town councils and the Isle of Wight Council. The current pandemic has shown that developing localism, whereby volunteers and village town based organisations are empowered, allows such bodies to act efficiently and responsively for their communities. Greater powers should also be given to allow such bodies to play a part in decisions that affect them, in particular in relation to planning.

Hi,  If elected I shall all my "Allowance" to create and sustain a Brighstone, Calbourne and Shalfleet Assembly representing all residents.  This would be as well as the normal councillor's duties as explained here

https://www.gov.uk/government/get-involved/take-part/become-a-councillor#:~:text=Councillors%20are%20elected%20to%20the,people%20in%20your%20local%20area.

Your County District Assembly would be everyday people volunteering and a very well checked random names from a hat method   The whole process would be as transparent as myself and the assembly members could agree with proper confidentiality for members. The Assembly would decide on my council votes and contributions if reaching a 2/3 or 66% majority. Modern citizens assemblies normally reach 75-90% agreement. In the case of no assembly agreement I could abstain or vote as I thought best. But would encourage agreement with further deliberation. A recent assembly making recommendations to the government on Assisted Dying reached an approximate 90% agreement. Please check #CitizensAssemblies , #MiniPublics , #CitizensJurys etc. You and they can help repair democracy for everyone

I am 27 years old and running for councillor of Carisbrooke & Gunville as the reform candidate.

I grew up on the Isle of Wight, having lived in the ward for 14 years, and I attended Hunnyhill Primary School, Node Hill Middle School, and Carisbrooke College.

I also helped out at a community club, teaching safe use of airsoft equipment to beginners/kids for 4 years.

Working at a local holiday park, I am known for my approachability and unwavering commitment to service.

I am passionate about improving local amenities, supporting island businesses, and ensuring every resident has a voice. I am excited to bring positive change to Carisbrooke & Gunville.

I’ve lived and worked here, and I’m passionate about preserving our rural villages while addressing the key issues affecting our community.

With my background in public sector finance and charity work, I have the experience to get things done and ensure our voices are heard.

Since I came second in the last county elections in 2021, I have continued to support residents in Arreton, Rookley, Godshill and Merstone and as a parish councillor for Chillerton.

I am ready to serve you on the Isle of Wight Council alongside a strong team of Greens at County Hall.

Despite growing up in a Conservative household, I always felt myself to be a guardian of the voiceless with strong environmental values.

As a life coach and therapist, I work with a wide variety of people. Working together for the greater good is my motivation for standing for local council, regardless of political beliefs.

My name is Claire Critchison (nee Whittington) and I am your Green Party Candidate for Chale, Niton & Shorwell (and Whitwell) in the 6th May council elections.

I'm a 6th generation descendant of this ward, having lived in Chale and Niton all my life and have brought my family up in the two villages.
I am passionate about the South Wight of the Island and support local clubs, projects, charities and community groups including being a Girlguiding Leader of the 1st Niton Rainbows and Brownies and Chair of the fundraising committee for Niton Primary School (FONS).

From being a community champion and speaking up for local issues I enjoy being part of our Island life and if elected I would represent residents to get the best outcomes for all. I work well with others regardless of their political alliances and would not be influenced when making decisions or voting on Island issues.

My career in Event Management for over 15 years with the Isle of Wight Council, working for Island companies and more recently running my own business ensures I have the experience, skills and ability to challenge others and ensure vital services and facilities which are a priority for the ward areas.

I've been selected by the Isle of Wight Green Party as the best person to represent the party for Chale, Niton & Shorwell. I'm very proud that they have put their faith in me and I hope this gives you confidence that I'd be great choice to represent our community at County Hall. I would be a strong voice on tourism, education, infrastructure, planning and prosperity of our Island.

This election will be a two horse race between myself and the Conservative candidate. Votes for Labour, Lib Dems or any other party won't decide the result. A vote for me is a vote for a strong, independent voice free to speak up for Chale, Niton & Shorwell. A vote for anyone else means a return to the same old Conservative complacency and our community not being heard.

I'm honoured to be standing to r

Dan is a teacher who moved down to the island to start a family. He is a young Dad with young children, and is deeply concerned about the state of the world. He says “I want to do everything I can to make it better for my children; their growing up and their future.”

Born and raised in Gurnard,  I was first elected as an Independent IW Councillor in 1995, and have represented Cowes West and Gurnard since 2009.

As Councillor, I’ve held key positions and given residents support in planning, the  environment, coastal protection, flooding, transport, waste, children’s services and public protection.

Outside the IW Council, I actively serve on three parish/ Town Councils, am Trustee of the Footprint Trust and magistrate. I chair the Isle of Wight Local Access Forum and since 2025 served as Chair of regional fisheries organisation Southern IFCA.

As Ward Councillor, I’m committed to being hardworking, transparent and putting Cowes and Gurnard first.

I was born on this lovely Island and have lived and worked here all my life. I have strong connections to Cowes and Gurnard. Myself, my husband and two sons, all born and bred here, strongly believe in the policies and strong common sense principles of the Reform party. Our Island and country is being overtaken and we must return to putting our British values and communities first, encouraging business growth and making work pay.

Sophus was born on the Isle of Wight and is a student at the Isle of Wight college. He serves as a Parish Councillor in Wootton Bridge. Sophus’s main priorities are fighting sewage dumping in the Medina river, plus improving public services across the island, particularly health and justice. “I would be honoured to serve the people of Fairlee and Whippingham Ward as their County Councillor”.

My name is Florence Sarlat and I am delighted to be standing as your Green Party candidate for Isle of Wight Council, representing Yarmouth and Freshwater North.

I came on the island by choice. I was drawn here by the beauty of the coastline, the friendliness of the community and the sense that this is a place still worth fighting for.

Prior to that, I worked for 20 years as a managing director for a bank, leading major transformation projects in Paris and London. In 2012, tired of managing only cost-cutting projects, I decided to train as a yoga and mindfulness teacher and worked with young people who have experienced trauma, injustice, and adverse childhood.

As a Green councillor, I would work across the issues that matter most to people here - not just environment but housing, transport, health, fairness, and the kind of community where people look out for one another.

Here are the things I am most determined to tackle:
- Cleaning up our water: I want to push for nature-based solutions that work with our landscape, not against it, protecting public health and supporting local tourism.
- Protecting our land and our character: Before any new building, we should be asking: can we restore and repurpose what already exists? Affordable, sustainable homes and community spaces can be created without sacrificing more of the landscape we love.
- Backing our local economy: Our farmers, artisans and independent businesses are the backbone of this community. I want to see the council do far more to support short local supply chains, keeping money on the island, and reducing food miles.

The things which make the Isle of Wight worth living on are fragile, and worth protecting fiercely. I am standing because I want to help keep this place extraordinary.

To do that I need your vote on Thursday May 7th.

Best wishes,

Florence

Originally from Teesside, I have lived and worked in London all my adult life, and in central Surbiton for more than twenty years. In a career working as a professional IT Project Manager for inner London councils I specialised in Parking, Finance and Housing, followed by a period working with Unison the Public Service union. I believe strongly that in order to make the most of the power of Local Government we must ensure it is well run, professional and honest in its dealings. It must focus on local issues and put residents at the heart of all decision making.

I have enjoyed being an active member of our local community for 31 years. With a history of “hands on” engagement in protecting our environment and promoting our beautiful area, I was asked by residents to stand for the Isle of Wight Council ward of Freshwater South.

Many will be aware of my role tackling our serious sewage problems head on with Southern Water, resulting in cleaner seas and rivers for us all, and also for supporting many local projects and community groups. I was instrumental in achieving the Conservation Area status for Freshwater Bay.

My priorities include protecting our valuable green spaces and wildlife, ensuring our rivers, estuaries and bathing beaches are cleaner, and that affordable development is in appropriate places with homes that locals can actually afford. I will continue to support our valuable library and West Wight Sports and Community Centre and to ensure our parks are safe, fun areas for our children to play in. I will focus on improving our NHS healthcare, enhancing local policing and tackling transport and infrastructure issues.

Above all, as an Independent Community Champion, I will be the substantial voice for residents to amplify and resolve their local concerns.

I am a retired teacher who specialised in working with children with special educational needs.  I am a resident of Freshwater and I am the prospective parliamentary candidate for Reform UK, Isle of Wight West.

I believe that successive governments have placed too much emphasis on education to degree level.  Encouraging and training young people to become the skilled men and women of the future will not only equip them to build a career for life but also help the country find the builders and engineers needed to solve the housing shortage we now face, as well as developing and growing our manufacturing capability as new technologies emerge.

I advocate a ban on any foreign fishing vessels entering British waters so that the pressure on fish stocks is reduced.  Rigorous scientific studies of stock levels and maturity should determine the size of the annual commercial harvest to ensure a healthy, growing population of fish for the future.  I am passionate about beach and small boat angling and a volunteer at the National Coastwatch Institution, Needles.

This country emits a tiny proportion of global CO₂ produced each year and we as a nation should not face the huge financial cost of trying to solve a problem we can have little influence over.  Net zero must stop as we will remain reliant on fossil fuels until real solutions for domestic, commercial, and transport energy needs can be developed. The number of people entering this country as economic migrants is out of control but the small boat crossings from France can be stopped immediately and legally. 

Those already here should be assessed quickly and efficiently regarding their right to enter, and either returned to their home country or allowed to stay, if appropriate, as the burden on housing, health services, education, and the benefits system is overwhelming this country.  A points system should be introduced to allow and welcome qualified individuals, who will benefit the UK, to enter the country and apply

I have lived in the Borough for 30 years and currently live in Surbiton Town Centre. I am semi-retired after a career that included spending 20 years working in Local Government for Westminster Council, providing welfare advice and support to vulnerable tenants. Until recently, I was a full time official for the public sector trade union, Unison, advising members working in London boroughs including Kingston. I currently serve on Kingston Council’s Standing Advisory Committee on Religious Education in Schools. I am passionate about tackling inequality through providing decent homes, safe streets and employment opportunities for all our residents.

As an Isle of Wight born and bred citizen, concern for all aspects of the Island is an instinctive priority for Peter. As a resident of Haylands, he can use his knowledge of planning and finance to ensure that this ward is well served and supported.

Peter sits on two national NGO forums, one directly with the Government Department for Energy Security and Net Zero and the other with the Office for Nuclear Regulation. Peter is a professional musician and a volunteer at the Isle of Wight Steam Railway.

Harry Eccles is a local Nurse, Trade Unionist and campaigner.

Harry is an addiction Nurse Specialist (in addiction) completing his masters in Advanced Nursing Practice.

He holds a national nursing leadership role giving him experience of overseeing complex multi-year projects and UK-wide teams with large budgets.

He is passionate about investing in local services, prioritising infrastructure and public need over profits, and protecting the local environment.

Harry has pledged to ensure all candidates have his contact details to discuss with him the issues that affect them most, so he can support them to create the type of change they want to see in Lake South.

I live with my family in the heart of Havenstreet village and already serve the community as Chair or our Parish Council. Most significantly I’ve spearheaded a campaign to get Havenstreet back on the bus network and represented resident’s interests in dealing with the all-too frequent disruption caused by roadworks in the village and surrounding area. This ward, one of the island’s largest, is a mix of larger villages and small hamlets. There are many thriving communities and businesses which deserve a representative at County Hall who will put their interests first at every opportunity.
My career in the British Army has given me wide experience of leading teams, managing change and budgets whilst being accountable for delivering the required outcomes. Just the skills needed on our council.
I volunteer as a Director/Trustee of the Isle of Wight Steam Railway, one of the Island’s top heritage and tourist attractions and a significant local employer. I fully understand the challenges currently faced by all Island businesses and the tourist sector in particular, a thriving tourist economy is absolutely vital for our Island. I strongly believe that our council must do everything it can to support all local businesses.
I am standing to be your Isle of Wight councillor because I believe that it’s time for change in County Hall. The traditional parties and the ‘Independents’ have had their chance. Reform UK is ready to step in and deal with the issues facing us, starting with the council’s finances which are in a dire state – fresh thinking and a push to further drive down costs, whilst delivering the services our residents need and deserve, is urgently required.
I’m ready to play my part in delivering the common-sense solutions that the Island needs. I’m right behind Reform UK’s core values – Family, Community, Country, and am prepared to work hard to serve all members of our Island community with honesty and integrity.

Whoever is leading the Government on June 9th, the Isle of Wight will not be their top priority, however as the Island’s MP it will be my single point of focus. Many people feel it is time we had someone representing them that will be solely focused on representing the Island because successive Governments have failed to understand the unique challenges we face. We know we have the ability to progress with a hand up, not just handouts. I believe we should be looking for an Island deal, an opportunity for real localism, to enable effective local governance and a chance to steer ourselves to a place of wellbeing and prosperity.

WELLBEING & PROSPERITY, Three Principles:

1. Economic -Building a strong, resilient and competitive economy
2. Social - supporting strong, vibrant and healthy communities
3. Environment – Protecting and enhancing our natural, built and historic environment

Economy:
• Small business micro funding and improved support.
• Cut VAT for Tourism.
• Circular Economy and the Island pound.
Transport:
• Back the feasibility study to determine facts regarding any fixed link.
• Work on solutions for rural bus travel.
• Create infrastructure for pedestrians and cyclists for able-bodied and disabled users.
• Road safety -20mph in residential streets and town and village centres.
Education:
• Make wellbeing as important as learning.
• Rebalance academic and vocational studies.
• Adjust the school terms for improved family life and educational attainment.
• Early Special Educational Needs diagnosis with ongoing support.

Health:
• Support the team at St Mary’s Hospital for improving and retaining services.
• Increase mental health provision for early intervention, especially for anxiety.
• Adult Social Care, lobby to end the post code lottery of care.
• More care for carers, so they can continue to support their loved ones.
• Raise aspirations for young people to choose a career in health

Housing:
• Build affordable rental pro

“I have spent the last few years disillusioned with politics and wishing Someone would stand and represents my views on the escalating local issues. Having spent a while hoping there was a local Lib Dem candidate I could support the next obvious step was to become that person!

“Now is a time of great political shift and change, and I would like to be part of the process that brings greater community improvements and social unity to our area."

I'm standing as your Labour candidate in Pan & Barton. I'm a local resident, and passionate about driving the positive change agenda of our new Labour government in our local area.
After 14 years of austerity, there is a big mess to fix and we are determined to deliver.
Alongside our three key policies on health and social care, housing, and transport, Island Labour will create more SEND places in mainstream schools using the Island's high-needs capital funding, and ensure primary schools receive a fair share of resources so every child gets the support they need close to home.
We will also tackle barriers to opportunity by investing in youth facilities, backing the island's Local Skills Improvement Plan, and helping young people who are not in education, employment or training into secure, well-paid work.
We will protect our unique environment by backing local renewable energy projects, helping Islanders access grants to cut energy bills, and pushing Southern Water and
regulators to clean up our rivers and coasts while holding the council to its climate commitments.

It has been a privilege to be the Isle of Wight councillor for Parkhurst & Hunnyhill Ward. It’s not just the area I represent—it’s where I’ve lived since 2007 and made my home.

Over the years, I hope I’ve shown that I am a councillor who listens to residents, takes up issues on their behalf, and sees things through.

I’m standing for re-election because there are many things I want to keep working on.
 
For our area, these are just a few:
•	seeing the upgrades to the prison estate roads through to completion
•	improving safety on our main roads and junctions
•	providing more equipment for the Hookes Way play area for young children
•	enhancing our local environment with improvements such as the planter soon to be installed on Hunnyhill near the existing bee hotel

For the whole Island, I am absolutely committed to ensuring the council prioritises poverty reduction, cost of living support, and truly affordable housing for Islanders to rent and buy.

Vote Jen Hughes for Ryde South East!

Local people FOR Local People

I am a passionate, tenacious and driven candidate who will stand up and be the voice for the people of Ryde South East

I am a current Ryde Town Councillor for Ryde South East, having been elected in first place in May 2025. I have worked hard to represent our Ward and have not shied away from scrutinising and pushing for clarity.

I worked tirelessly behind the scenes and helped save Oakfield Primary School from closure. I have also successfully challenged spending at Ryde Town Council, as a member of the finance committee and am not afraid to point out possible issues and money saving strategies.

I believe we need to vote for the best local candidate, and leave main party politics for the General Election in 2029. 
We need someone who has demonstrated genuine care and concern for Ryde South East, not a paper candidate or someone living elsewhere on the island, who may struggle to connect with people in our Ward due to living outside of it and not understanding the wishes of the people who live here.

I have aligned with the island Liberal Democrats, as I believe them to be the most genuine, compassionate and caring Councillors currently serving on the Isle of Wight. We need change and I honestly think these Councillors to be integral to this change, with their people centred approach, as, instead of doing TO people, these Councillors believe things should be done FOR people. I wholeheartedly agree!

I believe myself to be the best candidate and I hope you will agree by voting Jen Hughes on 7th May 2026.
Any questions, please do not hesitate to contact me, either by the link at the top to my Facebook page, or at:

jen.hughes@rydetowncouncil.gov.uk

Printed, published and promoted by Jen Hughes, 107 West Hill Road, Ryde, Isle of Wight

Thank you for reading.

My name is Sarah Morris. I am an Independent candidate for Shanklin Central, I am a Restore Britain member, and have passed their vetting, but they aren’t standing candidates outside of Great Yarmouth this time.

I believe we need something new and that other Islanders want change too. I am extremely frustrated and disappointed with what we currently have on offer,  so I have to try and make a difference.

We, as taxpayers, deserve a system that puts us first. A government free from corruption and cronyism. We need to stop our money from being wasted and given out in questionable contracts.  Most of those who currently run the country, and indeed those in opposition, have no idea what we are facing now and in the coming years.  Price hikes on food, energy, and mortgage rates, alongside possible food shortages and power cuts.  We need ordinary people with common sense speaking up for us.

A lot of families are on or very close to the breadline, and it absolutely wouldn’t be this way if the country was actually being run for us.  We need innovative ideas to deal with the many costs of lockdown, be it financial, the toll taken on our health system, or the impact on our children’s education.  Restore Britain have policy papers that cover many of the issues that need to be fixed, with more being added all the time.

The political class have been playing games with our lives.  Their bad decisions never affect them.  I am proud to be British, but we’ve lost focus and control.  We need to Restore the systems and get back to ones that work for us…Rebuild a country that we can be proud of, one we can be proud to pass down to future generations.

Locally we need people who are approachable, vocal, capable and willing to listen, work for and help all constituents.

I am always around, and am always keen to chat to anyone who wants to!

I am the only candidate who actually lives in the Shanklin Central ward, and I promise to do the very best job I possibly can if elected.

Why I’m standing for Shanklin South

Shanklin has been an important part of my life since childhood. I lived
in Ashcombe Court on Victoria Avenue and attended Shanklin Primary
School when I was very young. Much of my childhood was spent here,
and I have fond memories of travelling on the number 2 bus to secondary
school in Newport each day. My mum has lived in our beautiful town
for more than two decades and today, like you, I am also proud to be a
Shanklin resident.

I am standing because, I believe Shanklin South deserves a councillor who is visible, approachable and answerable to local residents. I believe these three things can only genuinely be achieved when your local representative is accountable directly to you and not to a national political party whose priorities may lie outside Shanklin.

If you choose to elect me as your councillor, my commitment to you is that I will stand up for you and only ever work in the interests of Shanklin South, without party politics getting in the way of that.

Ultimately, this election is about who will work in your best interests. But it is also an opportunity to send a clear message to Westminster that you are tired of this Island being overlooked. You can do that by electing someone committed to local priorities, who will be locally accountable to you, who uses and supports the same infrastructure and businesses as you and, most importantly, who is independent of party allegiance.

If elected, you are my boss

I'm a data scientist and consultant. I have lived in Shanklin for 10 years, and so understand the needs of the local community. My politics are firmly rooted in the centre ground. I grew up in Nottingham and have previously worked at the University of Southampton as a Senior Research Fellow, on a supermarket shop floor in various roles, in a call centre, and for a geotechnical engineering company. I've been a Labour Party member since 2010.

If elected, I shall:

•	Prioritise finding long term solutions to flooding in the town centre, and the closure of Leeson Road and other local roads
•	Back the Bay Area Place Plan so Shanklin, Lake and Sandown share decisions and investment fairly, boosting jobs and protecting the coast
•	Work with the national government to sort Island‑wide council funding and pressure on services, especially adult social care and children’s services
•	Push for better Island‑wide transport and connections; including ferries, road improvements, parking, and bus services
•	Support more local powers for the Island, fair funding, and building genuinely affordable homes
•	Keep a close eye on housing standards and press for firm action against poor landlords
•	Make sure Labour’s major increase in GP, dental and SEND services on the Island keeps moving forward

Every community on the Isle of Wight faces major challenges following years of decline under Conservative and (ex-Tory) Reform and Independent politicians. With the new Labour government’s positive change agenda, it is clearly essential that Councillors should work hard on behalf of residents to help improve their lives. If elected as your representative, it will be my top priority to be both available and accountable with a view to building a more compassionate and prosperous future for local people. I'm certain that, with your support, we can make a genuine difference in Shanklin South.

I am standing as an Independent, believing that local decisions should be made by and for those that live here and not according to the policy of a national political party.

I have served the residents of Totland & Colwell for the past 5 years and treated my position of Councillor as a full time role. I continue to hold Ward surgeries each Thursday at Totland Parish Office and variously at Our Place and similar venues, where residents can attend to seek my assistance or simply to make me aware of local issues needing attention. I cannot pretend to solve every issue but I always commit to do my best.

As a Totland Parish Councillor and Freshwater Parish Councillor I am uniquely placed to understand and take action throughout the whole West Wight - including key issues on protecting local greenfield sites such as Birch Close, Heathfield, Brambles, Camp Road and more, maintaining our local dental provision and securing them as NHS only, supporting viability of local businesses including Hursts and the Village Bakery, driving Southern Water to stop sewage discharges into our streams, SSSI sites and coastline, plus smaller matters regarding footpaths, retaining free on-street parking and public toilets and key services upon which we all depend.

My monthly reports to both local Parish Councils detail the donations I make of my Councillor Allowance to support our community - defibrillator training, prostate testing, laptop for Men-in-Sheds, youth services, establishment of wildflower meadows, wildlife support, Our Place and an extended list of local charities and support groups including Barnardo's and children’s support groups.

At Isle of Wight Council I have Chaired the IW Pension Fund since 2021 steering improvement from only 92% funded against liabilities to now some 160%, enabling a reduction in contributions by IWC this year of circa £2million that served to protect critical statutory services including Adult Social Care given our aged population.

Working with Em

I’m a local resident standing for the Green Party in Totland and Colwell, committed to making the West Wight a better place to live, work, and raise a family. With a professional background in healthcare, technology, and public service, I bring a practical, solutions-focused approach to local issues. I believe in improving everyday life through better infrastructure, protecting our environment, and supporting strong, resilient communities. I’m particularly focused on ensuring public money is used effectively, delivering real value for residents. My aim is simple: to represent our community honestly, make sensible decisions, and help build a healthier, fairer future for everyone.

As an Independent, I faced being shut out of decision-making or pressured to support policies I don’t believe in,

By joining the Conservatives under Cllr Ed Blake’s leadership, I can properly fight for our community’s needs where it counts – in the council chamber.

Frequent closures and poor maintenance disrupt daily life – we need proper investment. The ongoing situation with The Graben and Clarence Road is a farce.

Our beautiful villages deserve better support to attract visitors year-round.

Anti-social behaviour and drug-related crime need firm action. We must ensure quality affordable homes while protecting our rural character.

We mustn’t trade away local control for empty promises. Residents deserve a Councillor who’ll stand firm on what matters. Only Island residents should have a say in their day-to-day living and the future.  No other mainland authority can know what day-to-day challenges we face here.
